Initial Message

━━━━━━━━━▼━━━━━━━━━

'The two brothers navigated their way through the dense tropical foliage, the vibrant greens and browns of the jungle surrounding them, weaving an almost impenetrable curtain of plant life. Vergil meticulously took notes on the various flora and fauna that caught his keen eye, capturing details and observations that might contribute to their food stocks. Meanwhile, Dante, ever the more impulsive of the two, collected 'samples' of fruits and cleared a path ahead, swinging his machete with a mixture of frustration and determination. “How many more notes do you need to get?” he sighed, sweat trickling down his brow as he battled thick vines that seemed to multiply with every swing.

“Patience, Dante. You would be wise to understand the value of taking your time,” Vergil replied, his voice tinged with exasperation at his brother's impatience. He was clearly frustrated with Dante's lack of focus on their father's legacy. "We must preserve our findings meticulously—"

“Yeah, yeah, yeah. I get it. But we've been at this since sunrise,” Dante interrupted, exhaling loudly as he stomped through the underbrush. "I get that setting up camp is a necessity, but seriously. No one is going to steal our ship."

With a reluctant sigh, Vergil relented. “Fine. We will collect about five more samples and environmental notes before we head back. We can take our break at the beach afterward; I have some additional notes to write there as well. Will that work for you?” He raised an eyebrow at Dante, challenging him to argue.

“Mm... I suppose that would be fine. The sooner we can escape this muggy jungle and dive into that cool water, the happier I will be,” Dante said, a grin spreading across his face as he envisioned the refreshing sensation of the ocean waves lapping against his skin. “So, let’s finish up here.” He marched forward, ready to collect more fruits for the short break they'd be taking.

A short while later, after gathering several more information on edible plant locations and marking trails, they finally wrapped up their work in the jungle. They changed into their beach attire and trekked to an isolated peninsula, avoiding the prying eyes of their expedition team. As they set up camp, Dante looked around with newfound appreciation. “Hey, this is nice, Vergil. How did you find this corner of paradise and keep it a secret at the same time? I thought your infamous business-minded nature would have led you to set up several operation posts for your precious efficiency,” he said, grinning as he spread out a tattered cloth and set down the bags of fruit and salt-cured meat on it.
